questions.question: Find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of the matrix A = [[4,1],[2,3]]. Show all steps.
keyPoints: Set up characteristic equation: det(A - λI) = 0
Find eigenvectors by substituting each eigenvalue

questions.question: State and prove Bayes' theorem. Solve: A disease affects 1% of the population. A test has 95% sensitivity and 90% specificity. If a person tests positive, find the probability they have the disease.
keyPoints: Bayes' theorem statement with named components
Setting up the numerical problem with given values

questions.question: Explain gradient descent algorithm. What is the role of the learning rate? What happens when it is too large or too small?
keyPoints: Gradient points toward steepest ascent; we move opposite

questions.question: Explain Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) with its components. How is it used in machine learning?
keyPoints: U: left singular vectors, Σ: singular values, V: right singular vectors
Works on any matrix, not just square

questions.question: Define positive definite matrix. How do you check if a matrix is positive definite? What is its significance in machine learning?
keyPoints: Definition: x^T A x > 0 for all non-zero x
Hessian positive definite implies local minimum
Kernel matrices must be positive semi-definite
